grants.title: NSF Division of Materials Research: Chemical Exfoliation and Sorting of 2D Nanosheets
grants.summary: Development of liquid-phase sorting methods and synthetic techniques to isolate high-yield, chemically stable 2D nanosheets from bulk layered parent crystals.
grants.scientific_impact: Enables molecular-scale design of artificial superlattices and the stabilization of freestanding, single-atom-thick layered silicate crystals.
milestones: Established liquid sorting pipeline for layered silicates
Demonstrated robust NIR luminescence in exfoliated monolayer sheets

grants.title: DOE Office of Basic Energy Sciences: Novel Thin-Film Materials for Energy Applications
grants.summary: Synthesis and microelectronic characterization of quasi-one-dimensional van der Waals crystals for low-noise microelectronic interconnects and threshold switching devices.
grants.scientific_impact: Discovered the stability limits of charge-density-wave sliding currents and developed room-temperature current-controlled oscillators.
milestones: Demonstrated first stable current-controlled CDW oscillator at room temperature
Published comprehensive Materials Today review on 1D van der Waals crystals
Observed real-time structural transformation of TaSe3 nanoribbons via in situ heating STEM
